reviews.review_text:
Until the 1.0 full release of Path of Exile 2, this is my favorite ARPG for now

The campaign is amazing. Almost all classes feel good. Build variety is surprisingly high. Game looks incredible in so many ways and has some of the coolest armor sets in not just the genre but probably in all of gaming. Endgame is fun enough with a decent loop, you always feel like you make progress

But.

Early levels you feel incomplete, and some skills are WAY too overtuned or synergise with other things which makes you feel like you cripple yourself if you don't use it because they're so disproportionally stronger than the rest of your kit at that stage of the game. Lategame (around Torment 6+) the game becomes quite grind-heavy, and it starts feeling like you have completed your build and you are only adding minor stat upgrades or see number go up. There's also bug abuses that have made broken builds which can be exploited in leaderboards. Some classes can utilize resolve and other buffs for survivability which lets you push much farther than classes that just get oneshot (only relevant high-endgame). Most endgame builds are also basically a walking simulator, which becomes braindead and mind-numbing too fast especially with how grindy it gets later

Game is hella expensive. The seasonal challenges are OK, the cosmetic rewards are meh, the battle pass (yeah there is one...) is also kinda meh but for $10 cheap and good value with no useless stickers or something else. Cosmetics are stupidly expensive. I mean don't get me wrong, again they are unbelievably amazing and stunning, but you can't charge what Path of Exile does for an armor set when your game is $60 + $40 expansion. And they're class-locked, which is even worse value. It's such a shame that so few people will get to actually play and experience these amazing costumes, because my GOD they are beautiful, cool, creepy, everything!

Also, the game has some minor stuttering issues (mostly related to cache i believe. You should increase your cache size in your graphic card's control panel if you experience this)

But with all of that said it's still a top-tier game that will give you fun for a while, and i might come back next season

Honestly, when I started playing Diablo IV, I wasn't expecting much. I was still very disappointed by my experience with Diablo III. But from the very first cinematic, I was completely captivated. I was amazed by how much the game had evolved in every possible way — the storytelling, the visuals, the MUSIC, the gameplay itself — everything was leagues ahead of the previous parts of the series.

I finished the main Diablo IV campaign in just two days and absolutely fell in love with this entry. Right after that, I started Vessel of Hatred, and while the expansion wasn't bad, it felt like pure filler to me — nothing terrible, but nothing particularly impressive either.

Then I started Lord of Hatred, and once again I found myself glued to my monitor for an entire day. I even forgot to eat because the emotions completely overpowered my sense of hunger. I wanted to read every optional line of dialogue, discover more about the Diablo universe, and sit there with my jaw on the floor during the story twists and major events.

I even cried. Twice, Carl.

Could any of you have imagined that Diablo would make you cry? Because I still can't get over this incredible game and the unforgettable experience it gave me.

Blizzard really can deliver when they want to. It's obvious that this game was made with passion, and that the developers weren't afraid to make bold decisions that ultimately made it such a unique experience.

My rating: 9.9/10.


[table]
[tr][th]PC Specifications[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]RAM: 32 GB DDR5[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Storage: NVMe M.2 SSD[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Resolution: 2560×1440[/td][/tr]
[/table]

The game itself is not that bad , the huge problem is that the monetisation looks like a Free To play . 
You have rewards blocked behind a wall of season pass.
DLC is expesensivs , and much worse is that some talent or non-DLC classes are blocked if you don' t buy the DLC, this is not acceptable.

The GAME is good. Better than people give it credit for. The monetization is horrible and predatory. I recommend buying the edition with both DLC expansions (on sale if possible) and ignoring the micro transactions completely.

Finally in a good enough spot that I'd rather play this than D3, though the fact it took nearly 3 years to get here is ridiculous.

It's a fun, casual, brain-off ARPG that I can genuinely recommend to fans of the genre now. I'd still rather play PoE (especially since it's free), but sometimes you just want to switch your brain off, y'know?
Put 50 hours into the new season/expansion in a week. Started on whirlwind barb, then swapped to a minion build around Torment 10.

Gripes:
Way too many cosmetics shoved in your face

The barrier to entry is too steep, playing the base game without the latest expansion is practically pointless since you don't get access to any of the new content

Builds and metagame/data mining are a fraction of PoE's

Trading is non existant unless you are in the very very high tiers. It also sucks, the main trade website is laggy as ♥♥♥♥. I much enjoy PoE's trading system, where even during the act's/levelling you can get decent enough drops to chuck in a public stash tab to sell off. None of that here.

Honestly after 150 hours I gave up on this game.

The game crashes every 5 mins, to every 30 mins for me. Between just standing at my stash to being in the middle of a run and it freezes for 3 secs and closes. I gave up doing/using the multilevel dungeon runs, which I had only completed one of by paragon level 240 due to crashes. It came down to I was afraid to use any keys for content until I fixed my issue.

I googled my issues, part being system error 10016. Tired everything to fix but nothing worked. 

A few days ago I did a full system wipe and new clean windows install. Still same issue, and this is the only game I have had this problem with. Between no cross character progression with there new system end-game for the trees, and the crashes. Just not worth the frustration.

It's fun to play with friends. Generally, enjoyable experience.

But the reason I can't recommend the game is because the Blizzard is the greediest company out there. You get the game for such a price and they just block so much content behind the paywall anyway. And the prices are like crazy high especially given the game is already expensive in the first place. How about skins for 20-25 EUR, plethora of overpriced battle passes etc. Even skills for base classes are hidden behind the paywall. 

Optimization: some places have pretty big stutters, not sure why. Drops to like 30-40 fps. Otherwise optimized well. Playing with path tracing and all on ultra. 

Voice acting: sometimes feels like AI, my friends also shared this impression. Not sure if i's just how the voice was recorded, or they actually use AI for some content.
Singleplayer RPG with lag. Shallow experience, feels like you're playing a mobile game.
Horrendous mtx and pricing model, gameplay feels floaty, cinematics and music are well done

Probably the best ARPG in the market for a wider audience regarding the gameplay, but this is blizzard so of course they have to make everything else a frustrating experience.

The good:
-Story
-Graphics
-Aesthetics
-Class design
-Enemy design
-Content
-Fast paced combat
-Great skins
-Bosses
-Endgame

Overall, like I said, it's probably the best ARPG experience with flashy combat and a never-ending content, multiple ways of expanding your skills and gameplay.

The bad:
-mircotransactions are off the roof 
-RNG system in item drops and rerolls is garbage
-Max diff introduces the most creative way to die: Oneshots (most of times from things you can't even see) regardless of your toughness (armor/hp/resistances)
-The game needs a huge ton of QoL changes like when you want to transfer all your items to your cache, you have to do every item individually (2026 gaming btw)
-The newest DLC has locked away CORE features like the cube 
-The story is overall good, but in the latest expansion they tryharding way too much to make us sympathize with lilith, like she didn't slaughter people for no apparent reason other than her delusional "purpose". Like she didn't smile over corpses clearly enjoying seeing people suffering. They tried to play the "it's deeper" without making any sense.

Anyway, good game, good experience, most problematic stuff are irrelevant to your playthroughs, buy it even full price.

Overall:
8/10

People think it's weird to 100% games sometimes, but I do enjoy the challenge and seeing all the things games have to offer.  

This game beat me.  It's disheartening to see how difficult the achievements are in this game.  This isn't just a grind or a mastering of a skill.  It's life that this sucks out of you.  They want you to play this game for hundreds of hours.  And yes, I'm aware that you can play across many seasons and it'll eventually add up, but I'm not interested in playing a game like an MMO for a long time.  I like to finish my games and move on.

In typical Blizzard fashion, they have also destroyed content from the base game as they add expansions.  Old crafting systems are no longer available.  For example, alchemy and herbs.  You get nothing but veiled crystals at least as far as I got.

There are also broken quests that people have been posting about for years.  Of Pestilence and Pests being an example.  

And the worst insult of all, to a gamer like me, is that there are unattainable achievements.  Even if you were to spend the hundreds of hours they want you to spend in this game, you will never get 100% today without cheating it in.  There are achievements that are simply unattainable.  They just did a major patch and have not fixed it after a very long time.

I'm not even mentioning how often the store comes up within the content of the game.  There's currency that drops in the base game that you use to buy items that are locked behind paywalls.  You get the currency doing normal content, but you can't use the currency on most of the items it buys without paying for the pass.

UI screens for the equipment looks also have store items listed in them as locked.  The store is on the map page.  The store is on the character login screen.  

The game has alerts to new items, and yes, you guessed it... there are alerts for the store items to make sure you look at them.  And you have to let it load or it won't clear the alert.  The alert also appears on the main UI of the game, which you can't hide either.

I do not recommend this game.  It played like Diablo 3.  So if you had fun with that... then you'll have fun here.  Other than that, pass.

as a vetran player of diablo series I can say that the 4th installment of the game is a huge success and no the game is not a pay to win ( u can buy cosmetics but it is by choice ) 
Also very interesting mechanics that they have added make the game feel fresh with every season .
I love diablo since i was little and this is my review for the game . If u like ARPG this is the game for you .
10 out of 10 i hightly recommend the experience .

The game is suppose to be played as co-op? but the server ping issue is not suitable to play even in solo mode. Did they make it worse? I used to get 105 ms to 169ms but now it's more than 270+ ms which is unbearable to play and hinders any kind of gaming experience. How mighty blizzards server are failing people.

Long ass rant below.

Blizzard is such a scummy company, they've locked some skills for the base game classes behind the LoH DLC, forcing you to pay for the expansion if you want to access them. They've also delisted and bundle VoH in with LoH, so that owners of VoH can't get a discount on LoH and so they can charge more for it, AND THEN reworked the loot system around the DLC EXCLSIVE Horadric Cube, and it really messes up the endgame.

I mean, the game is great, don't get me wrong, the story's sick asf, and the gameplay itself really fun, but now its gotten to a point where I can't recommend it because of the constant and blatant "♥♥♥♥ you" Blizzard is giving to people who bought their ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ FIFTY QUID GAME thinking they would get to play something where our time and money is respected by the game and devs, and instead received the Money Grabber 3000 they've given us.

Anyway. Here's the TLDR.

It's a great game, if you're thinking about it, buy it on discount. But the monetization is so ♥♥♥♥. Also be aware that some skills for the basegame classes are locked behind a forty quid paywall.

no matter how visually cool the game looks, no matter how addicting and fun the gameplay is, no matter the good story, characters and overall music is, the thing that kills the vibe for me is countless bugs, predatory battlepasses, expensive cosmetics and some annoying mechanics. like how is it that since the new expansion release a simple thing like exploring Skovos is still not fixed in achievements? getting stuck in encounters and losing progress because you can't do anything besides relog. not to mention a lot of warlock visuals just clutter the entire screen.

wow, why did i purchase this game even? 
after seeing poe 2 new league, i dont want to play this anymore at all.
making your dlcs cost money says all about this and the "diablo 2 resurrected" thing mi bob is all a way to suck up money and they did successfully with mine. 
having to lag so much when getting back to town is such a tedious process and i dont think my pc is bad. 
always having to press shift to stop and shoot manually is so bad even with so little space for spells, i hate it. 
path of exile does it better even after so many years that this game gets support over so many lenghty years, it dissapoints me
The Drop nerf since the recent x-pac, adds way too much grind to the game. In torment 1, a hell tide chest dropped 3 blue items. Thats all, THREE BLUES. Screw this i got better things to play.

It requires that I need to pay for not just the base game but for DLC as well to play the game that I already bought on PS5 WITH ALL EXPANSIONS. You are way too greedy. I already paid more than 100 bucks for this game, ain't buy the dlc pack second time and I requested a return on steam. L decision Blizzard.

1) There's no f-ing way to create a new isolated character. you inherit gold, map progress, paragon, etc. I wanted 1 character to do the campaign and 1 to free play.
2) Clearly a game designed to stretch out and waste time instead of just letting you play. Hundreds of little design decisions jumped out to me and I feel phyiscally sick after only 70 hours.
3) Cash shop always in your face.
4) Interface sucks, as well as just about everything else.

Suffocating microtransactions aside, there is a really special game in here.

The story surprised me a lot, i was not expecting to be this invested in the world and the characters, even more so this being my first diablo game. Every cinematic is just absolute cinema, and normal cutscenes do not disappoint either.
The gameplay loop is super addictive, and the mechanics are simple enough that you don't need a guide to make a viable build, but they are also deep enough that you can immerse yourself in each class to try to push it to it's full potential.

If you are arriving late to the party (3 years into the game), just know that this game is in it's best place since launch, they have improved every aspect of it immensely, and I can only hope they release a couple more big narrative dlc's.

Um jogo muito bom, a trilha sonora é absurdamente boa e divertido demais, fico só meio triste por skins pagas, não afeta o jogo, mas seria muito mais legal senão tivesse.
Tenho que elogiar o elenco de dublagem do jogo, e a dlc pra mim tem a melhor bossbattle do jogo todo.
O jogo tem sim missões genéricas e alguns problemas de narrativa, mas no geral o universo e a história são legais.
Gosto de simplesmente sair fazendo ordas, eventos, dentre outros e a variedade de habilidade com as builds, expande bastante a durabilidade do jogo.
Recomendo!

Diablo 4 is everything I wanted from a modern ARPG and more. The atmosphere is dark, immersive, and beautifully crafted, bringing the world of Sanctuary to life in a way that feels both nostalgic and fresh. The combat is incredibly satisfying, with every class feeling powerful and fun to play.

What really keeps me hooked is the constant progression — whether it’s hunting for legendary gear, experimenting with builds, or jumping into events with friends, there’s always something rewarding to do. Blizzard has continued improving the game with updates, balancing, and new content, and it honestly feels better than ever right now. 

The visuals, music, and cinematic storytelling are top-tier, and the endgame offers a ton of replayability without losing that classic Diablo feel. Whether you’re a longtime fan or brand new to the franchise, Diablo 4 is an easy recommendation.

One of the best RPG experiences I’ve had in years.

Why would they make a game that only does seasonal stuff? Makes no sense. All that grind and effort just to start over ??? Make it make sense. If you want a game that makes you start over after every 2-3 months, this is the perfect game for you .... 


Goodbye Blizzard ... and Peter
Pay to play classes you've already paid for and constant disconnects.

They made a base version of the late game mandatory DLC cost as much as the base game. And double the price for the full version of the DLC. BS upon BS. This POS company should've been dеаd when the Immortal was released, but it still manages to tickle the balls of people that have money and don't have a functional brain.

pay to play new classes, pay to play new skills on EXISTING classes. disgusting ok blizzard have all my money to keep up with the game. so greedy do not play this game go buy yourself new shoes or something.

I spend more time being disconnected from the game then actually playing it. The live servers are not stable. Spend maybe 20 minutes on one of them before getting kicked off, getting an error message that the game crashed and then a 3.2gb download that takes 5-8 minutes before I login again. Literally unplayable and I think the game is slowly destroying my SSD.
Nerfing all the fun mechanics with the next update. The devs clearly hate their player base having fun

Paid full price for a full game, then said full game was downgraded to force players to buy a DLC which is the price of a full game. Also ♥♥♥♥ uber lilith. I shouldn't have to look up the one very specific build for the one class that even stands a chance against her, only to realize that I have to start the game over just to beat a boss.

This game sucked 3 years ago. Doesn't anymore.

It still isn't on the level of POE but it's finished, fun at all levels and has a pretty decent endgame. POE is too overwhelming for me and this strikes a nice balance between shallow character building and so deep you drown. Story rules too.

Fun from 1-70 and beyond.

Tried game again hoping for some changes and found that a 3rd of skill twig was paywalled, D4 BAD, just go try POE2  (or wait till its free at the end of this year) at least skills won't ever be paywalled in that game.

Кастуешь 6 одинаковых спеллов и видишь только их анимацию/звук, и на этом выбор заканчивается

На экране какафония, сложно разобрать что происходит (играю за варлока)

Welcome to Layers of Random.   Feel the excitement as you do four extremely similar endgame activities(spoiler: one doesn't have a timer!) and pray for what you want.  

Want those gloves? Do some random events and hope a random key drops.  Then, go kill the boss and hope your random key drops your random item. Didn't get what you wanted? Its ok, you can't craft keys or guarantee their drop from anything, just hop on that random treadmill again!

After two expansions it finally feels like a 1.0 game with poor endgame.  Battle pass, cosmetics, $50 every time a new class comes out, ugggh.  Just spend your time on a better game.

Just brought this game to chill, it takes up 134Gb on my steam deck, and now it wants another 70Gb to download an update !!! Its just too much man lol. Always another bloody update. Cant even play offline !!! So when i go bush or to the farm  i cant play, and when im on the public transport dead zones i cant play. So whats the point? I am uninstalling.

Dear multi billion company, I believe you have the resource and time to fix one of your best incomes, by removing the memory leaks and upping the performance. This is not a game worth 70eur with both expansions.

"Greetings,

Battle.net Account: XXXXXXX
Action Taken: Account Closure
Violation: Game Exploitation

We've recently found character activity on this account that violates our Code of Conduct (https://battle.net/support/article/42673). This includes, but isn't limited to, cheating, botting, and exploiting game mechanics which harms the game environment or the intended player experience."

Blizzard banned my account and all they provided was the above email. I was not engaged in any activity that would have violated their "Code of Conduct" but I sure as hell can't tell what it is they thought I did based on the email they sent to me.  Now I am left trying to prove my innocence via email because Blizzard doesn't even have live customer support anymore. Best case scenario, they recognize it was some mistake and restore my account after they decide to get off their asses. The best/worst part is I (and many others) are not really interested in the online portion of D4. It is fine but I don't really care about it and do not interact with it and now I cannot play the game due to some imagined violation of a rule set that only needs to exist because of ancillary systems that do not provide any benefit to me.  Blizzard can take their made up ban and shove it up their ***. I am not going to accept this nonsense.

Lords of Hatred campaign was very good. 

The loot changes are a nightmare for anything remotely casual and a huge step backwards. Cannot recommend if you have other games you also enjoy playing.

Just came back after a long rest from this game and now it won't even load my new saved game, it just crashes. I'm using the Druid as my new champ. At first it let me play a bit then ran out of video memory, then I started again it let me play a bit then it crashed, loaded the save game again and now it crashes as soon as the new game is loaded, like wtf man. The game was working good before I added the DLC Vassel Of Hatred. Worst part is now I can't even refund or play the game I spent alot of money on, this is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ it should be taken of steam for good.

Honestly, it's a lot of fun. It's getting a downvote for one reason: 

The post-campaign Lilith re-battle. 

This one battle breaks the conventions of the rest of the game entirely. It doesn't belong. For one simple fact: Lilith has an auto-kill ability that you must dodge. And it is not an easy dodge. In fact, it's extremely difficult. The attack comes as a ground swipe in multiple directions. You have to identify the pattern when it's telegraphed, move into the right spot, and then move in the correct pattern to avoid multiple swipes in opposing directions. Think dodging traffic with the cars going every which way they want. On top of that, there's not just one pattern, but several, so you have a split second to identify it and go the correct route. Do you want to make this a bit more complicated? What if she had multiple attacks that all telegraphed in very similar ways that are extremely hard to perceive with everything else going on the battlefield. So now you have to not only identify the correct pattern, but also make sure that the attack is one of the ones you have to dodge. If you dodge the wrong kind, and then the right one comes along? Sorry, your evade just may be on cooldown and you're dead. 

This sequence is initially done at break-points in Lilith's health, but it's not consistent in any way. She'll sometimes do just two of these maneuvers, then go back to fighting. Sometimes she'll do four or five in a row. There's no pattern to it. This is somewhat manageable when she does it the first couple of times and she flies up off the field (where she's now locked in health no matter how many DOTs you've put on her), and you can move around relatively freely. But during her last about 1/3 of her health, she'll do these while on the field. Lilith's hit box is enormous, not only blocking the view, but if you click in the wrong spot and attack her instead of moving to where you want to go, congratulations, you've died because you didn't move out of the way. 

Let's say you somehow manage this carnival of blood pressure raising game mechanic and do defeat her, with a bit of luck, good timing, and a lot of tries so you can train yourself to recognize and dodge those attacks. Fantastic!

Now she's on to phase two. Yes. You've spent a good fifty runs throwing your head into the wall, finally think you've gotten it, raise you hands in victory that you managed to do it, only to go into a second round where she has, yet again, more attacks of the same level of auto-kill insanity. Only this time you can't run into it repeatedly and learn through masochistic doggedness, because you'll only get to this part of the fight perhaps one out of ten or twenty times. You may not even get to see the attack that killed you to recognize it enough to learn to dodge it by the next time you get there. 

All of this would be perfectly find in a different game. But it's entirely unfitting in Diablo 4. There's nothing else in the game that comes close to just ignoring the game mechanics as this does. Even at a Paragon 200+ Paladin tank build, you can't survive it. On top of that, the game does not, as other reviews have mentioned, run smooth enough. So even if you get good at the dodging and patterns, there's a decent chance you click your evade to move into the right spot and just... don't. And then you die, knowing you clicked it, knowing you did the right thing, but that the game decided that you didn't get to live. 

This fight just simply doesn't belong here. If the game were built around dodging mechanics more, and were more fluid, and there were degrees of training in previous fights that built up your player skills to do this, then maybe it could. But it doesn't do any of those things, and is thus simply a massive failure from a game design standpoint. 

Oh, and by the way, you have to beat it to progress through Season 13's ranking system. So you also can't just ignore it. Hope you're in one of the classes that has a niche build that allows you to ignore the mechanics, because otherwise, your season's over.
Bought this game to play on steamdeck completely offline. And still have to connect to internet and deal with network lag for offline game…..

After finishing Crimson Desert, Diablo 4 became the game that kept me hooked. Yeah it's repetitive, but in a way that makes you want to keep playing. There's always a better item to find or a build to impove, always one more dungeon, one more upgrade, one more piece of loot. Easy recommendation if you enjoy ARPGs.

At this symbolic playtime, I can confirm that I would recommend Diablo IV. While undoubtedly imperfect and, in some respects, inferior to its predecessors (with special recognition for its horrendous monetization), it remains an epic modern entry point to the ARPG genre.

I like the Diablo IV play mode on the easiest.  So far I am a 38 rogue, and there's lots of unique and legendary drops even on the easiest setting.  I like the backgrounds and the eerie, wriggly, and positively demonic dungeon horror hellscapes!

The game is fun when it works but I can't play for more than a couple hours without it crashing and taking my system with it.  My system is MORE than capable of running it yet it is the only game in my library where I experience this issue.  Check the forums and you will see there are many other users experiencing this.

[b]I recommend this game![/b]

Currently, I’m playing through the storyline (a campaign with an narrative focus), and I’m very satisfied. I always look forward to returning to the game and continuing the journey, which is a very good sign.

The graphics are well done, and the world feels vast and immersive.

Thumbs down for the monetization, though.

Blizzard made the best games of my Childhood.
I started with Diablo 1 at 10 years old and Diablo 2 became my favorite game for many years after that.

Considering that it's so deeply disappointing that Blizzard can't seem to make good games any more.

Diablo 4 is an outright embarrassment.
The Gameplay is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.
The Storytelling is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, it should be told through the gameplay not with a million cutscenes and dialogues.
And yes the cutscenes and dialogues are all ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ too, I quickly found myself spamming the skip button as much as possible. Only some of the interactions with Lilith were Kind of cool, but could have been condensed to a single interesting dialogue or a cinematic instead of bloating it with fetching blood trails or whatever that ♥♥♥♥ was.
The graphics and the game engine is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ too.

It's an entirely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game Blizzard. Please trash everything, and when it's time for Diablo 5, try to take inspiration from Diablo 2 and Path of Exile.

Been a player since Diablo 1 & 2. Love the new version as well but I came online specifically to leave a review for the update/patching of this game. Every few times when the game updates via Steam, it takes more than 30-40 minutes. I am running on a 2Gbps fiber link and it downloads at near 1Gbps speed for at least 20 minutes and then patching for another 15-20 minutes. As a working adult with family, I barely have any time to play in a week and when I actually sit down to play, this garbage starts updating and takes forever. Absolutely incredulously disappointed with this pathetic useless ridiculous way of updating and patching. After all that billions of dollars spent in production, this is what they can come up with? Not going to spend a single cent on Blizzard games until they fix this garbage.

Diablo IV is an Absolute Blast
If you love hacking and slashing through hordes of demons, Diablo IV is a must-play. It takes the dark, spooky vibe of the classic games and mixes it with modern, smooth gameplay. It’s addictive, visually stunning, and incredibly fun. Whether you’re a long-time fan or totally new to the series, you’ll easily lose hours to this game. 9.5/10!
It's a lot better than it was at launch with numerous enjoyable end game activities.

Addon Review First. (Skip it and read the last paragraph for the actual review)

I've enjoyed every Diablo. Each felt different, yet they kept this "Diablo" feel. I like this one, its different. The open world, the mounts, the graphics. The size is enormous but it comes with the 4k graphics. I think the biggest issue for me, is how the developer/publisher broke the game in pieces to monetize it separately. Once we used to pay some 29.99 (later on 39.99) etc. and you had the game.
Now days...half the game (half the classes) in 3 tiers so some of us can pay more, then some expansions with also 3 tiers (standard, heroic, legendary), so they can "milk the cow" more, and a shop with overpriced skins.
Have I ever spent on skins? Sure, on CS where I can later on sell them, and repurpose my money.
D4 is great yet is not WoW. It will not be here 20+ years, and I feel the company is over focusing on profiting.

(Actual Review Here) Get it on SALE. (when its 15 to 20 bucks) The base game is great, beautiful graphics, nice gameplay, the usual Diablo series fun stuff. However, don't rush for the expansion. Play it and enjoy it at your own pace, and buy the expansions on Sale too. I wouldn't bother with the expansions now, unless you have money to waste. Then go for it.
An excellent ARPG and a fun social game. It has come a long way since launch.

LORD OF HATRED BVWAS GREAT BUT IT RUINED PERFORMANCE.......game runs like a wet paper towel now thanks blizzard between this and the garbage 10 yr for overwatch i think im done with your games.
want a game that looks great plays great and runs great go play PATH OF EXILE and if u want a pvp alternative to overwatch go play DEADLOCK or THE FINALS.

I recommend this game.  Diablo 4 has evolved from a hollow launch into a mechanically superb blockbuster ARPG, boasting a sharp loot economy, polished combat loop, and meaningful endgame progression. The game is a fascinating contradiction, while the Vessel of Hatred expansion as a whole feels mid, the narrative campaign within it stands as one of the best gaming campaigns ever made. It delivers exceptional pacing and atmospheric weight that temporarily salvages a broader world prone to artificial padding, repetitive fetch-quests, and an overlong runtime. However, the overarching experience still stumbles by trading the stark, solitary existential dread of Diablo 2 for a stale "power of friendship" tone that clashes with its grimdark roots. The handling of sacred lore will irritate traditionalists, especially given the baffling, conspicuous absence of Diablo himself. Despite these narrative shortcomings and the uneven DLC packaging, the massive post-launch updates and stellar campaign storytelling make it a flawed but highly recommended experience for any ARPG fan today.

Daiblo IV:

I bought Diablo IV because Skill Up recommended it like a trusted dealer whispering, “Don’t worry bro, this one’s good.”

I originally bought Diablo IV near launch, but my friend group collectively developed PTSD during the Microsoft acquisition drama, so nobody actually played it. Much like my high school dating career, I sat there excited, emotionally available, mildly aroused… and completely alone.

Sigh.

Eventually, I started fresh with a friend who practically lives inside Diablo IV, only to discover the game politely punishes you for not finishing the campaign first. Nothing says “play with friends” quite like Blizzard placing a giant “not yet, idiot” sign in your path.

The funny thing is, I never really wanted to “play” Diablo IV properly. I just wanted to create the entire cast of He-Man. Barbarian? He-Man. Necromancer? Skeletor. Sorcerer? Evil-Lyn. I spent nearly 40 hours assembling my Avengers of Eternia before realizing seasonal progression makes this an absolutely terrible idea.

Another sigh.

So eventually, I powered through the main campaign solo. And look, the story isn’t bad. Quite the opposite, actually. But it felt like trying to appreciate fine dining while desperately needing to take a ♥♥♥♥. I just wanted to reach the endgame already.

Yes, I know you can skip the campaign. But my mother did not raise a coward, a shortcut taker, or a certified moegoe.

And somehow… I still haven’t reached the endgame. Because now I have another friend who wants to play on “♥♥♥♥-you difficulty” while I’m sitting there at level 50 with the combat capabilities of a wet Woolworths packet. My Paladin hits enemies with all the force of a disappointed substitute teacher, and my other level 70 characters apparently need to replay the DLC story again.

At this point, Diablo IV feels less like a game and more like a long-term relationship with a very attractive toxic person.

Of course, I’ve already heard the Path of Exile crowd explaining how Diablo IV is “too casual” and “dumbed down.”

My brother in Christ, the Path of Exile skill tree looks like the FBI used cocaine to map the universe. I have a job. I cannot spend six hours studying a skill tree that looks like the Milky Way just to make lightning hit slightly harder. So Relax, Gareth. You’re not a warlord. You’re unemployed with RGB lighting.
Surely there is room for both of these games to exist in our world?

I recommend Stockholm syndrome with loot drops.

🖖🏻
Brom.

[h2]An alright ARPG but a horrific Diablo game[/h2]
Read on to find out but my recommendation unfortunately: Skip to VoH if you're narrative driven and watch a lore video.

With the release of Lord of Hatred I decided to stick my toes back in and see D4 through to the end, I've always been a fan of Lilith's persona and what she stands for as the mother of creation, the tone for this cultic mommy enabling her children as they blossom in her image was absolutely mesmerising. Anytime she was on screen was absolutely wonderful in a trance kind of way, like I was part of her following just like her children - You can understand the weight of her character. But for some reason I never stuck the landing the first time round, forcing myself through the story back on release and only making it to Act 5.

So how is it now? 

[h3]Built to get through it, not to experience it.[/h3]
The phrase above about sums up my Base to LoH journey - You'll become overgeared, overpowered and you just won't care.

Base Diablo 4 campaign has decent opposition with its antagonists in Elias and Lilith. You get introduced to other Evils down the line but they don't quite stand toe-to-toe like the demons in Diablo 3 regarding weight of significance or terror: Diablo, Azmodan, Belial. And as a result, it becomes what I like to describe as "the Scooby-Doo syndrome", you're just constantly playing catch up to the real villains of the story and it takes so damn long. You'll go too and fro around the world as you seek allies to help you determine their next steps, what their intentions are next and there's just no eureka moments as they're pretty much always 2 steps ahead. 

Out of my journey, the only significant thing I can remember is the ending to Act 2 for both what I expect from Diablo with its actions and ethos (huzzah!) but too for how painfully bad the boss fight was after what just happened. 

To follow that, just how short the last push is once you reach the Gates of Hell. It is epic for sure between the last push and abundant delusion on play, its the setpiece to be in the driver's seat for. The environment? Amazing. The cinematics? God tier. Execution? Once again. just doesn't live up to the severity of what's implied. [spoiler]Especially since we somehow make it to the cathedral before Lilith does, despite seeing her in eye-shot of it before we are? Whatever I guess.[/spoiler] 

And this brings me to the tag line: the game is tuned right now to get through it rather than experience it. 

Edit: Doing some reading, it seems I failed to understand requirements to unlock Torment levels are NOT locked behind Skovos. However, as a result of its poor representation to tell me this, I'm still not impressed there isn't a measure in place along the lines of: If player is level cap, hasn't unlocked Torment and is murdering bosses in 3 seconds, maybe we should nudge them to the Torment requirements. Give me the chain to go unlock it Blizz... I was under the impression I couldn't...

So if you don't unlock Torment, Expert and eventually Penitent will not impose you, nothing will terrify you and nothing will matter, no matter how much the story tries to make you believe that. 

Base Diablo 4 campaign is bad, its just not good nor interesting and murdering everything effortlessly only adds to the weightlessness of the experience. You won't care about how powerful or imposing the foe is in the cinematic as once the game snaps back to gameplay you'll just backhand them to a molecule of their existence. 

[h3]Vessel of Hatred ~ It doesn't get better, but at least the story does[/h3]
Vessel of Hatred is no better for it, in fact, I never died during my 7 hours or so from start to end unlike Base where I only died due to bad RNG on elites and just being murdered before I have time to react. Instead, with the addition of Mercenaries and their traits, you're even more powerful either dealing more punishment or bolstering your defences. 

HOWEVER. Markedly better is the story, at least in my opinion. Neyrelle plays a more integral part, witnessing her struggle that is placed upon her and how it tears her apart inside. VoH isn't about chasing evil or being gated by the next big baddie that you'll just mow down, instead it focuses more on your allies and about finding what will help this infection to bring your companion peace. And extensibly, how VoH progresses to LoH as Mephisto is understandably, from this point on, the big concern now.

Storywise I felt I cared more here than base since there wasn't really a goal upfront, there was no expectations in truth and as a result, I didn't find Vessel underwhelming. That said, I now understand why Vessel of Hatred was so poorly received and why people argued their value proposition when Lord of Hatred released, demanding a discount or even suggesting getting the story update for free. This should've been a complete package as the story in Vessel is INCOMPLETE, to imagine playing this if LoH wasn't available would piss me off too, VoH ends on a big cliffhanger after all your efforts and if I had to pay to see how it ends, I'd be ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ about it too. 7-8 hours isn't a bad runtime narratively for an expansion, especially in todays market *humblegrumble* but once again, Vessel doesn't do a good job of being [i]Diablo[/i].

[h3]Lord of Hatred ~ Satisfying conclusion, still mechanically inept [/h3]
Wrapping up LoH on Torment 3, I will say that this is what D4 needed and was a pleasant end to the game. However it still suffers from being too easy and now conclusively, the whole D4 experience just has no weight or threat. There are little to no mechanics and don't encourage anyone looking for a tough time to give the story a lick of their time. Get to the end as fast as possible to tailor your experience.

[h3]Why write a review and why negative?[/h3]
I like Diablo 4, I do. It just never matches the hopelessness and dark fantasy grittiness of its lineage. I do wonder if that's more of a tell and don't show in some regard that I'm missing here as I think back to the intro cinematic of the Wanderer in Diablo 2 torching the inn. That modern presentation is what's holding back the Diablo I'm looking for which in turn says: "Go play 2" which I've reluctantly ever done so perhaps that's my problem. 

Back on track ~ LoH does reach the heights it needs to and is the pinnacle narratively, it pays off to have gone through it all than to skip to the final Expac,  appreciating Lilith's part in LoH and how Mephisto hijacks the chase. But any hoping that gameplay or boss mechanics scale with the experience are going to be sorely disappointed outside avoiding rare one-shots.

Are ARPG's about story? Not as a mainstake, sure no. But Diablo has a wonderfully dark world that anyone into dark fantasy should lap up. And unfortunately, the run upto LoH where the game clearly wants you to be isn't worth the journey as I'd hoped for. At the very least, you'd be better off watching a video retelling the story on YT than slogging through the game if you're narrative driven and start from Vessel of Hatred. 

As for endgame, not been there but to take probably about 50 hours to get to this point, it's not a hallmark of: You can finally play the game! It just could've done more in those 50 hours...

Make matters worse, as an achievement hunter, at time of writing, 100% is impossible to do. Small problems for big impacts.

To wrap up;
- Game didn't kick my butt and torment quests aren't part of the journey upon hitting level cap
- Due to lack of difficulty and short boss fights, investment in developments were woeful
- The whole experience is mechanically inept
- Story takes too long to get good
- At least the builds are kinda fun albeit scaled with Torment in mind
- Music and atmosphere doesn't meet previous titles
- Where the heck are my 12-string guitars?
- Looking at PoE2 coverage makes me think I might have more fun there...

If you are a Dad, if you want to wind down at the end of a day, for a half hour or so- this is for you. If you are easily persuaded to buy cosmetics for a game, and you are broke already, well better skip it :-D The game mechanics are straight forward, tons of guides out there as well. It has that tripple a price tag for an overall good visual expirience (probably the best). However, is it the best ARPG? I'm unsure about that.

How do you make hell boring? Ask blizzard. Definitely not buying another game from this pos company.

bought d4 between poe leagues. mistake.
movement is ass. the mount gets stuck on pebbles, wont climb anything, dismounts you mid-combat and puts itself on cd so you walk back the half mile on foot. miss a waypoint and backtracking is its own punishment.
verticality is somehow broken in a top-down game. ledges everywhere you cant actually use, invisible walls forever.
world is huge and empty. ride 40 seconds, kill 3 guys, ride 40 more. they built a whole open world and forgot to put a game in it.
every 5 minutes a cutscene grabs me by the throat. dont care about lilith. dont care about the npc with the sad backstory. let me click on demons.
killed a pinnacle boss afk on a minion build. took my hands off the mouse. skeletons handled it. i was making coffee.
stuff that doesnt suck:

looks sick, art team carried
paragon board is interesting but shallower than it pretends
minions are viable to level which is rare, even if it makes the afk thing worse

not recommended. play poe2.

Game is ass, why? cause game is ass. Therefore, game is ASS.
Who the hell locks core mechanics and skills from the skill tree behind an expansion. Admittely, I got the latest expansion, had a bit fun, but man the end-game is just barebones. I skipped story, cause I don't play ARPGs for that. There is so little build diversity that quickly it just becomes meh... You hit a wall, game pumps out Ancestral Uniques left and right, the only thing you really do at end game is trying to reroll your affixes on gear to hit better or rather, GREATER affixes. If you value your money, don't get this gabbo. Rather go play D2, D3, Grim Dawn, Poe, LE, anything that's not D4. 
It's true the game has gotten better with Lord of Hatred, but it's still just a baby. Game should've been released this way.

TL;DR: Game is ass

I tried to power through this game before posting the review but I really dont know if i can find the energy to keep going. this game might be a 7 to 8 out of 10 for anyone whos never played any other ARPG.  

But for me personally after playing POE 2 this game just has a bunch of things I think POE 2 does better. levelling up in POE 2 feels like your doing something important building into what you want to be. leveling up in this game feels a lot more boring and stream lined. basically all the abilites in the game can get 15 points worth of upgrades from leveling up...which basically means leveling up is a matter of you putting another point into your "go to ability"

The ui gives off mobile game vibes. you cant run but you get a horse in the open world. so when your inside your stuck going super slow which can be annoying during missions where you need to back track which there is a few. the art style is bland the graphics are passable for sure, but most of the environment is very basic looking with drab looking filter tacked ontop of it. 

The open world makes everything worse, in pretty much all of the other games in this genre theres linear levels that are somewhat large and open to allow for some exploration. in POE 2 a bunch of missions will have 1 main boss and 1 optional boss you can do and enemies for you to fight everywhere in between also checkpoints you can teleport between after discovering them. in diablo 4 a large chunk of your mission will  be you going across the open world and IF YOU FEEL LIKE IT you can hope off and do some fighting in between locations.  

The open world combined with the slow story telling kind of goes against everything diablo 1 built as the groundwork for the whole franchise and genre. diablo 1 was so fast paced and streamlined people coined it an "action role playing game" diablo 4 pretty much has the least action in the whole genre out of all mainstream action role playing games. the main story is so poorly done that they just give you the option to skip the story.

The main story its self is fine. id even say its good but theres a lot bogging it down. asside from the open world slowing you down from completing the story at a reasonable pace. sometimes the story will just go plain slow even not including all the walking around you have to do to get to the main story quests. prime example being this really slow bit in the desert where you have to follow this old man through a sand storm that basically does no damage. it feels like the devs expected you to have no HP at all during this section because they stop every 40 seconds or so to allow you to use infinite health potions from the old mans camal I wasnt exactly playing a tanky class. i was playing sorcerer and was tanky enough to regen health fast enough to never die in the sand storm so it was pretty muich just wasting my time. also much like in POE 2 this game shows a lot of characters youve just met go through the worst moments of their life and get super sad and upset or even die. the diffrence is diablo 4 does it a lot more slowly and dramaticly going as far as to show differant camera angles and sometimes cinematics and really lingers on these moments as if they expected me to remember the names of all these characters i barely know. BUT ill give credit where credit is due, sometimes a boss will have a really cool introduction with some cool lines of dialog to hype up how strong they are. 

which brings me into the next point. most of the bosses in this game get tee'd up just to not be strong at all. i never died this entire 13 hours i played the game. I didnt use a guide and I turned the difficulty up to expert. I couldnt be bothered to turn it up more because you have to exit the game to main menu turn up the difficulty there and than load back in. but pretty much every boss in this game a push over your probably going to have a harder time fighting the random enemies in the open world. Im not trying to brag if you think I am. Im not good at video games in general so i only mention how easy it was for me to warn you if your a long time gamer this game is very hand holdy and dosnt push you to your limits at all. 

overall im giving this game a 5/10 It falls flat for me but I do respect the effort put into the game. its not a fun game for me but not from a lack of trying. they tried a few new things like the open world and camera angles and stuff during dialog and it took away from the experience for me but I cant acuse them of slacking off thats for sure, its an ambitious game but it never really came togeather for me.
Game is wonderful. Don't listen to the haters. Even if all you play is the campaign you'll get your money's worth, and the regular seasonal content is great!

Paywall the skill tree for classes you paid for is just trash. They overhaul the system and then charge you for it? It was reworked because the original wasn't cutting it. That is part of game development. Many games release, receive criticism for something, developers fix it, then players are happy. Not, we fixed it but you have to pay to utilize the full rework. Limiting NEW mechanics and content is a completely legit practice, this is just greedy as hell. Adding/changing a few skills is not a new mechanic or content, it already existed before and the fact it was reworked is the clear indication that it needed to be done because what existed was inadequate. Would not have bought it if I knew this before hand but I am well over the 2 hours to refund so this is the best I can do. Sure as hell won't be buying any cosmetics from a company that uses trash practices like this to squeeze money from its player base.

This is the first Diablo game I've played, but not the only ARPG I've played. I own only the base game, and the monetization scheme and ads they run everywhere on the UI is truly off-putting. Shown but paywalled content everywhere, notifications to guide you to buy useless (and often ridiculously looking) cosmetics, shop that almost forces you to buy the dlcs. Combine that with the ground effects from the new classes other players are using (making you unable to see if you're standing in a poison puddle), dropping swords and revenge ghosts, and the whole experience becomes obnoxious. Which is a shame, because underneath all the obnoxiousness is a good arpg with a well made world and interesting lore.

I'll probably get the dlcs when they're on a hefty sale, but until then it's Grim Dawn (a great arpg, not just good) and others for me.
Campaign is really good. Cinematics just perfect.Game is nice but mostly i prefer the Campaign. After that the game is okish.Too much grinding.

The game is high tier garbage. Blizzard as a company cant fix problems that have been in the game for years and its damage scaling is so off that you can walk around and get one tapped or eviscerated by some mob out in the open world. Its optimization is garbage for how long its been out. I just can't play this trash anymore, i tried so many times to give it a chance and hope for something good, but its generally just bad.

I would like to start off with saying I did enjoy playing through the game. The end-game experience though was just like every other. Slowly chiseling the things I enjoy out of a build just to have to focus my damage on one single skill for the sake of end-game optimization. Fun game play for the most part but it will eventually make you destroy everything creative and fun.
Took them a few years but the game is finally in a good state and very much worth playing now

Before I write my review let's me just say straight off the bat - The monetization in D4 is NOT OK!! £20+ for a cosmetic outfit is no even close to acceptable, these things should be just part of the game and earned through rewards etc.. Second the DLC price is on the heavy side and some parts of the skill tree for CORE characters are locked behind it's purchase This also is NOT OK!

With that out that way if you don't mind paying the DLC prices and ignore the in game cosmetics the game itself is good. There are 16 difficulty levels with almost everything available to play from difficulty 5 onwards. Drop rate and quality increase the more you push your build up the levels, but if you prefer not to then there is no need (unless you are chasing pretty pointless seasonal objectives). 

There are plenty of build types and play styles to pick from, some of course being easier than others. Plenty of content, with a selection of endgame activities to get engaged with.

If you can bypass the shocking greed and 100% ignore the in game cosmetics shop then D4 is very enjoyable indeed.

If you’re playing a build that uses summons, there’s one main boss and one side-quest boss in particular that glitch and become invincible, or the script fails to register their deaths, making it impossible to progress  and they still haven’t fixed it.

Not to mention a quest that’s impossible to complete because you have to give a potion that can no longer be crafted, so it’s impossible to complete the game 100%

new expansion is the first thing theyve done right in a decade. beat mephisto game bugs during cutscene wait 5 minutes to be able to log, yep restarted all the way back at beginning of quest reminding me its still a blizzard pile of ♥♥♥♥, smh.

You're expected to buy every piece of DLC to actually get the full Diablo® 4™ experience©.
No sets in the base game + paywalled non-boss-specific uniques is an interesting designer's choice, definitely.
Well, on the positive side, it's still high-quality and fun enough.

Devs trying to sell the same DLC twice (first time, we bought it separately, now they offer it to us again as inseparable part of new DLC). 
New season and patch hiding parts of character and season progression beside the paywall of new DLC: you need to grind more if you don't have it and lose access to some of your skills. This sounds like a worst practice from f2p trash, but it is actually here, inside a 50$ game.

I think if you were to judge the game now, as a product that is being sold for 50$, I think it is a fair price. The game is pretty good now, with a lot of build variety, end game content is pretty reasonable, and it plays very well and feels very fun.

That isn't excusing the fact that Blizzard is a terrible company that engages in some really bad practices, and the game took too long to get to this point (and their pricing with DLC was abhorrent) but the game is good enough for the price they're asking for and it's a genuinely good buy on sale.

Any live service games have ups and downs based on balancing, buffs, and nerfs but particularly with the latest updates (Lord of Hatred) Diablo is in a really good place. I've had fun grinding gear with friends.

As always, it's a value judgment if you think the game is worth your money or time. I've gotten so much value from my money even if I waste $20 in the shop now and then. Looking forward to more content.

I get why some people hate it. Some of the decisions Blizzard made (and continue to make) are downright obnoxious. I hate them as a company. However...130 hours in and combat that makes every other ARPG look pathetic. I've tried them all over the past 20+ years, and Diablo was always the best. Diablo IV is even more satisfying than D2, and I didn't think that would ever be bettered. So yeah, garbage company but magnificent game. Sorry.

Just finished playing over 30 hours of this game. I have Vessel and Lord of Hatred. I have to say this is a pretty solid game. I have played Diablo 2 and Diablo 3 (at length). I have to say Diablo 4 is my favorite by far. 

I constantly find myself deviating away from the main quest lines to explore the map, run a dungeon, conquer a stronhold, or simply because something interesting.

The mount system, open-world style, speed of levelling, the ability to skip the story line, and power level your friends are all much needed additions to this franchise that the previous games lacked.

I still wish they didn't require internet access or have monetization (from what I can see this is only for cosmetics).

I enjoy coming home from work to put 1-2 hours into this game each day and find it sadistically satisfaying (lol). It scratches the ARPG itch that I have been missing.

Not sure if my OS stats will show in this, but I am running Pop!OS cosmic and this runs great, with minor tweeks.

I have already recommended this game to family and friends and will keep doing so.

Solid 9/10 from me!

The age of D4bad is over. The time of the D4good has come.

Memes aside, I think the game is in a much better state now than how it was at launch. It doesn't have the build or itemization diversity or complexity that PoE does, but that was *never* going to happen, and that's fine. It's a great "log in, run a few dungeons, then log out" chill game. It should definitely be noted though that Blizzard is clearly moving towards the D3 gameplay loop, so if you didn't like that play style 10 years ago, you probably won't like it now.

I'm definitely not a fan of set charm bonuses though. 5 piece sets are wildly overpowered that your first immediate goal when reaching endgame should be to get your class's 5 piece, so being shoehorned into using the same 5 piece set for every build in that classjust reduces build diversity more. It's probably unlikely that they will nerf it though, given the design patterns leaning more and more into D3.

I've never played diablo before. As a first time player, this game is freaking awesome. I love the builds, the story and progression, the combat. It really is such a fun game and I keep learning more stuff about it.  

I don't really care about the cosmetics other than what's dropped so I'm not too attached to how my player looks! I think it's all badass for what it is and the upgrades that come with everything is really cool.  I appreciate having to really think about what I'm going to wear and how that's going to affect the character's abilities and battling.  Everything really is so fun. I haven't even finished the campaign, but I did get the expansions and am seeing the DLC drops while I'm still going through the main campaign. 

I started the game on expert and found that it's still pretty easy given the abilities, they really just take longer.  Surely after this campaign I'll explore other characters but for right now, this game has me hooked!

Get this game when it's on sale though - it can get a little pricey (understandably so - what they're delivering is pretty awesome).

Lots of love, lots of love, and lots of leveling up :)

With the Lord of Hatred expansion, Diablo 4 has become the best game I've ever played in my entire life and I've played a lot of games. Literally the best - I don't say it lightly, no exaggeration here. The end-game systems are mind-blowingly deep, the new reworks are high quality, and the whole game direction feels incredibly well thought out. 5/5 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

From a business standpoint, I wish they hadn't paywalled those passive nodes from the skill tree rework for those who for some reason want to continue to play the base game, and also would offer like a 10% discount to all previous expansion owners to avoid negative review bombing. Perhaps a better rating and sentiment could actually convert into more money for them, even despite the discount and free nodes.

Late Game progression is now completely paywalled behind a DLC (best skills, loots and freaking Horadric Cubes), feeling scammed. Uninstalled after finishing the main storyline. Game's not worth the extra price of the DLC, going to POE2 instead.
Monetization strategy is truly terrifying... diabolical at very least.

My review is simple: If you are a new player and are coming in with all of the DLC, it's fantastic and easily one of the best ARPGs ever made. It's consumed more of my time in the last 2 weeks than my literal full time job (60 hours worked vs 72 hours played, based on today). It's that good. Gameplay is incredible and the drip feed of dopamine hits between levelling and gearing is perfectly tuned to be maximally satisfying - seriously, this game is like a drug. 

For reference, I am currently Paragon 200 on my necromancer and running Torment 10 relatively comfortably, dropping to Torment 9 for speed farming. Playing on a Lenovo Legion 5i with the mobile RTX 5060 gpu, it runs great on medium settings at 1440p. I could probably push the settings harder, but I'm sitting on a couch with a laptop, not really worried about maximum fidelity like I would be on my desktop at 4k.

The caveats: 

The story kind of sucks balls. I have a lot of complaints with it in terms of writing. Still fantastic production quality, as expected of Blizzard, and I'm sure some people will like it. Vessel of Hatred is good, base game is absolutely abysmal until the very end where it somehow goes from ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ to mediocre, and Lord of Hatred just pissed me off. (Tried way to hard to be grimdark, ended up just making nothing feel like it matters, but not in a poignant way, just an empty miserable way. It did not feel like a Pyrrhic Victory earned at great loss, it felt like a relief that I finally got to just play the fun part of the game again).

If you already bought/played the game pre-DLC, some content you previously had is now semi-paywalled with the skill tree rework, and the game explicitly expects you to have the DLC and to engage with the DLC systems - War Plans, Talismans, and Runes are all crucial to the satisfying endgame progression Diablo 4 has, and it will feel awful if you are going in with just the basegame, worse if you already experienced the game before Lord of Hatred's sweeping rebalances and overhauls and didn't buy it. 

Lastly, the monetization is blatant and terrible. It feels like they took all of the cool weapons that would normally be used for Uniques and Legendaries and instead they are selling them to you. THere is very little variety in terms of armor and weapons unlocked through gameplay, but if you go look at the cash shop you can get all kinds of cool flashy ♥♥♥♥ that is wildly past the level of anything you earn through gameplay. The battlepass is also terrible, stupidly slow, and does not give enough cosmetics to feel worth either the price of entry or the level of grind necessary to unlock it. 72 hours of gameplay and I still have 4 more items to unlock in the last "reliquary", in order to unlock the last piece of armor - and if i don't, I'll just own a mismatched set of armor forever and never be able to complete it. Miserable. I wouldn't even have the premium battlepass if it didn't come with the deluxe lord of hatred bundle, do not waste your money or your time on it.
Great story and addicting gameplay loop
They have fixed most of the gripes from game release, although the cash shop is still egregious

Holy cow, the bosses in the campaign are so miserable. Get ready to deal with some of the worst hit boxes ever as you get knocked around the arena and watch your attacks hit and miss bosses seemingly at random. And the health pools are pointlessly large, so you will have ample time to enjoy these ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hit boxes. Bosses also typically have random teleportation abilities, so have fun using your ultimate just to have the boss jump out of it and leave you with a minute long cool down.

Just play Path of Exile 2, or even Diablo 3. Bosses in an ARPG should be fun, and these are the exact opposite. These bosses make you feel weak and powerless, and when you finally beat them after ten to fifteen minutes of running around being annoyed by horrific hit boxes, all you feel is relief when you finally kill them. No satisfaction, no rewards, just relief that you won't have to deal with whatever ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ was going on.
It's Diablo. Kill things and get loot explosions for dopamine!

Game got better than initial launch. Recommended for casual players than other ARPGs. Meta sucks. While there are a lot of classes and builds available, the need to play certain classes or builds if want to push far into higher difficulties is very frustrating and meh.

I get that the new loot system is supposed to pull in the player to do more with their builds but for personally it became a headache to deal with. I loved diablo 4, but the changes in this new expansion just dont do it for me. The new torments they added are basically the same 4 torments previously but just more broken up; it became way more tedious just to progress. Blizzard putting so much reliance on the hoardic cube to get high end gear killed the game for me. I love grinding games, i have 2.5k hours in destiny 2, and so on; but the cube system gives me such a migraine to deal with, its just not for me anymore. its awesome if others are enjoying the new changes and i respect that, but these new changes just made the game ultimately not fun for me anymore. Plus the amount of money they want for cosmetics, expansions, battlepasses that dont even give you the full amount of silver that the bp originally costed back and so on; im just out of patience with blizzard to do better. They had years to make things better, and instead ruined it for me. POE 2 here i come.

The game is better than at launch even though its current state should have been the launch one and it took 3 years.
The bugs are never ending. They regress to things that people complained to before like ground effects that kill you after you completed a dungeon. This was removed and its back. Spell bugs that just dont get fixed and new ones consistently.
Also they have now secretly reduced the XP gain for their already crap reliquary system so much that you will have to pay again because you wont be able to get enough tokens through gameplay. Even though you already paid for the reliquary.
So yeah its probably better to get this on sale if you want the story and then move on because they are getting greedier and lazier.
Patch 3.1 is apparently going to be the "no fun allowed patch." Mythic changes are dumb as hell, along with far too many nerfs to almost every class.
